text: The patient is positioned with the right arm initially stretched out and then repositioned below body level. A Veress needle is introduced, and the stoma site is sealed. Three ports are marked away from the stoma site. The surgical team checks with a telescope before docking the robotic system, utilizing a three-arm technique without the fourth arm. The peristomal hernia site is visible.

The surgeon begins the hernia repair using unipolar scissors on the right and bipolar forceps on the left, reducing the omentum from the parastomal hernia. Dissection lines are marked on the screen as the surgeon enters the retro-rectus space using monopolar scissors.

The surgeon develops the retrorectus space, moving superiorly towards the lateral edge where the Linea semilunaris is encountered. The surgical team transitions into the tower plane, focusing on achieving a tension-free closure with a unilateral approach.

The surgeon uses articulating scissors and forceps to dissect around the stoma, revealing neurovascular bundles above the scissors and the shiny peritoneum indicating the correct TAR plane. The instruments facilitate precise ninety-degree dissection, creating a buttonhole defect as the surgeon moves laterally to free the planes.

The surgeon closes the hernia defect using PDS StrataFix sutures, aided by the articulating robotic arms. The lateral space is obliterated by suturing the lateral edge of the mesocolon to the muscle plane. A tape is used to measure the dissection size, and the mesh is cut accordingly. The buttonhole around the colon is closed from inferior to superior. The mesh is introduced to lateralize the colon using a modified Sugarbaker technique with retro-rectus placement of polypropylene mesh.

The surgeon sutures the corners of the polypropylene mesh in place, opting for suturing over glue due to the robotic approach. Following the mesh placement, the posterior sheath and peritoneum are closed, marking the final phase of the surgery. A final check is performed to ensure completion of the procedure.
Thank you very much. This is Dr. Arun Prasad from Manipal Hospitals New Delhi.
